seoTitle: Stewart Range vs Stirling — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Stewart Range and Stirling. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Stewart Range vs Stirling: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stewart Range and Stirling.
heading: Stewart Range vs Stirling — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stewart Range, South Australia and Stirling, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stirling has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Stewart Range (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stewart Range or Stirling safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stewart Range has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Stirling scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stewart Range scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Stirling scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stewart Range or Stirling?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stirling has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
